# Security Checklist: Runtime Implementation Options and Contract Decision

**Purpose**: Validate security requirement quality for runtime dependency footprint, no-network/no-install bootstrap constraints, supply-chain handoff boundaries, public support-claim limits, and artifact assumptions that could force XPLAT-003 into an unreviewed security model.
**Created**: 2026-06-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, and the rejected small per-platform binary record all required to record dependency and bootstrap footprint before selection? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-017, Plan Technical Context]
- [x] CHK002 Is installed-cache no-install behavior a pass/fail gate rather than a weighted preference? [Completeness, Spec FR-016, Research Installed-Cache Gate]
- [x] CHK003 Are post-cache package installs and package restoration explicitly prohibited for the selected runner? [Completeness, Spec FR-016, Plan Constraints]
- [x] CHK004 Is the initial plugin install or update fetch separated from prohibited post-cache package restoration? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications Session 3]
- [x] CHK005 Is XPLAT-003 required to receive selected and rejected candidate supply-chain implications without XPLAT-002 choosing first-release controls? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-017, Research]
- [x] CHK006 Are evaluated-runtime dependency and output assumptions classified explicitly enough that vendored packages, embedded runtimes, native binaries, generated artifacts, lockfiles/manifests, or unknown package-manager expectations cannot become implicit security controls for XPLAT-003? [Resolved, Spec FR-017, Data Model Supply-Chain Implication Matrix]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is the selected runner prohibited from relying on `npm install`, `pip install`, `uv`, `brew`, network package restoration, or equivalent per-user setup after the plugin cache is populated? [Clarity, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK008 Is the command contract clear that helper dispatch resolves from the installed plugin payload/cache root, not repository source paths? [Clarity, Contract Helper Dispatch]
- [x] CHK009 Are source-checkout and generated-payload probes limited to supplemental evidence rather than installed-cache security or reliability proof? [Clarity, Spec FR-005, Research]
- [x] CHK010 Are runtime-info/preflight fields specific enough to expose selected runtime version, plugin root, source-vs-installed context, capabilities, and prerequisites for support without claiming validation controls? [Clarity, Spec FR-015, Contract Runtime Info]
- [x] CHK011 Are missing prerequisites surfaced as deterministic diagnostics instead of instructing users to install packages during runtime? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Contract Missing Prerequisites]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do spec, plan, research, data model, contract, and quickstart consistently avoid selecting supply-chain controls? [Consistency, Spec FR-003, Research, Data Model]
- [x] CHK013 Do public support-claim boundaries consistently exclude README, docs-site, marketplace metadata, changelog, release notes, and public support promises before XPLAT-007? [Consistency, Spec FR-019, Research, Quickstart]
- [x] CHK014 Do the artifacts avoid changing public support-claim surfaces while still allowing decision-record, candidate-evidence, and handoff wording? [Consistency, Spec FR-019]
- [x] CHK015 Does the XPLAT-004 handoff stay limited to selected runtime, command contract, fixture parity expectations, and adapter records rather than supply-chain policy? [Consistency, Spec FR-018, Spec FR-018b]
- [x] CHK016 Does the no-shell/no-`jq` runtime boundary remain a contract requirement rather than a security claim about the final implementation? [Consistency, Spec FR-014, Spec FR-020]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Can reviewers objectively verify that the selected runtime model needs no per-user dependency installation or network restoration, with actual installed-cache invocation proof deferred to XPLAT-004? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-006, Spec Assumptions]
- [x] CHK018 Can reviewers objectively verify that XPLAT-003 receives dependency footprint, manifest/lockfile behavior, generated artifact types, build/release path, scan path, checksum/signature/SBOM/provenance feasibility, trust root, native/build-time dependencies, build inputs, runtime/install execution risk, maintenance posture, and evidence gaps? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK019 Can reviewers objectively verify that public support-claim changes are absent from XPLAT-002 outputs? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-007, Quickstart]
- [x] CHK020 Can reviewers objectively verify that XPLAT-002 remains a decision spike and does not implement runner behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec FR-020, Plan Scope]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Is a candidate that requires package installation or runtime restoration after cache population required to fail the installed-cache gate? [Coverage, Spec FR-016, Research]
- [x] CHK022 Is unavailable installed-cache evidence handled as a host-specific evidence gap rather than implied success? [Coverage, Spec FR-005, Research]
- [x] CHK023 Is a documentation/probe conflict required to remain an evidence record with scoring impact rather than silently accepting a runtime claim? [Coverage, Spec Clarifications Session 1]
- [x] CHK024 Are public preview, release-note, and support-claim changes explicitly rejected until XPLAT-007 validates native release readiness? [Coverage, Research Public Support Claims]

## Notes

- Initial checklist pass found one true gap: dependency and artifact assumptions were listed in the XPLAT-003 supply-chain handoff, but not classified tightly enough to prevent vendored package, embedded runtime, native binary, generated artifact, lockfile/manifest, or unknown package-manager assumptions from becoming implicit security controls.

## Rerun Verification - Loop 1

- [x] CHK025 Does FR-017 now require assumption status for candidate dependency and artifact assumptions without selecting first-release controls? [Resolved, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK026 Does the data model now classify artifact assumption type and status for source-only, generated payload, vendored package, embedded runtime, native binary, platform-specific, external package-manager, and unknown cases? [Resolved, Data Model Supply-Chain Implication Matrix]
- [x] CHK027 Do unknown or unverified artifact assumptions now become evidence gaps for XPLAT-003 instead of accepted controls? [Resolved, Data Model Validation Rules]
- [x] CHK028 Do plan and research now frame dependency/artifact assumptions as XPLAT-003 implications rather than security controls or XPLAT-004 implementation permission? [Resolved, Plan Constraints, Research Supply-Chain Handoff]
- [x] CHK029 Does quickstart validation now ask reviewers to confirm evidence-backed versus assumed dependency/artifact shape and unknown assumptions as evidence gaps? [Resolved, Quickstart Artifact Review]
